layout_and_geometry.container: aspect-video w-full max-w-7xl mx-auto relative overflow-hidden bg-gradient-to-br from-[#0a0f1a] to-[#1a1a2e]

phases.name: Assemble
phases.action: Particles gather, central node scales up from 0 to 1, orbital nodes spawn and scale, cyan connection lines draw sequentially.

phases.name: Hold
phases.action: Central node breathes/pulses (scale 1.0 to 1.05), slow rotation of the entire orbital system, drifting particles.

phases.name: Dissolve
phases.action: Cyan lines retract, nodes shrink to 0 or shatter into particles, particles disperse.

phases.name: Restart
phases.action: Empty void state, resetting for the next loop.

webgl_requirements.lighting: Cinematic. Ambient light + strong directional light to highlight the Gold and Cyan. Maybe a PointLight inside the central node.
webgl_requirements.materials: MeshStandardMaterial or MeshPhysicalMaterial for nodes (metalness: 0.8, roughness: 0.2). LineBasicMaterial for connections.
webgl_requirements.particles: PointsMaterial with additive blending, moving via simple sine waves in render loop.
webgl_requirements.substrate: A flat plane or grid helper with cyan color and low opacity (0.1) on the XZ plane below the nodes.
webgl_requirements.labels: HTML/CSS overlays mapped to 3D coordinates (using manual projection of Vector3 to 2D screen space), styled with Space Grotesk, absolutely positioned.

css_fallback.trigger: Mobile viewports (max-width: 768px) or if WebGL context fails.

visuals.central_node: A sharp diamond/rhombus CSS shape (using transform rotate-45) in #D4AF37 with a pulsing box-shadow.
visuals.orbital_nodes: Smaller sharp diamonds/squares with glowing cyan borders, positioned around the center using absolute positioning.
visuals.lines: Faint 1px cyan CSS borders connecting nodes, possibly using SVG overlays.
visuals.particles: A few small absolutely positioned <div> dots drifting slowly via CSS keyframes.
visuals.animations: Simple CSS @keyframes for pulse, float, and opacity fades. Skip the complex 16s assemble/dissolve loop to save battery and performance.

accessibility_and_testing.aria: Add aria-label to the container explaining it's a 3D visualization of the Universal Contextual Intelligence Matrix.
accessibility_and_testing.prefers_reduced_motion: If (prefers-reduced-motion), pause the GSAP timeline and display the 'Hold' state statically.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Ensure 'three' and 'gsap' are installed (yarn add three gsap).
2. Create a React component 'UCIMVisualization' that acts as a wrapper for the vanilla Three.js class.
3. The Vanilla Three.js class should accept a container ref, initialize the scene, camera, renderer, lights, and start the render loop.
4. Implement the Hex Grid substrate as a flat plane with a hex texture or simply a GridHelper with cyan color, low opacity.
5. Add the Central Node (Solid Gold #D4AF37, angular geometry like Icosahedron) and 8 Orbital Nodes (mixed Pyramids, Octahedrons).
6. Draw connecting lines between the center and orbital nodes.
7. Implement the drifting Gold particle system using THREE.Points.
8. Implement HTML overlay labels using Space Grotesk font. Project the 3D position of each node to 2D screen coordinates in the render loop to position the HTML labels absolutely.
9. Create the 16-second GSAP Timeline for the assemble -> hold -> dissolve -> restart sequence.
10. Add Intersection Observer so the animation only plays when the container is in the viewport.
11. Build the CSS/HTML fallback inside the same component, conditionally rendered for mobile (e.g. window.innerWidth < 768) or WebGL failure.
12. The CSS fallback should be a clean, geometric layout with zero border-radius, floating dots, and a central pulsing element.
13. Add the required data-testid attributes to all key elements for testing.
